T0377.title: Travel-Lodging Advance
T0377.definition: Upfront expenses for travel and accommodation that fraud rings advance to debt applicants, to be deducted from the final payout.
T0377.description: In organized loan fraud schemes, intermediaries cover the travel and operational costs of participants traveling to designated cities. This advance, known as the 'three covers,' is a tactic to attract and lock in participants. Once the loan is approved and disbursed, the intermediary deducts these costs, often with interest, from the participant's share of the fraudulent proceeds.

references.title: Decoding the Auto Loan Fraud Industrial Chain
